metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Scenario 2: add an independent diff-scope assertion (e.g., query Gerrit REST `/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/files` and assert the file set matches only the stacking agent's commit, not parent-story files) rather than relying solely on the bot's own robot comment to prove correct diff scoping.
findings.finding: Scenario 2 (Stacking) asserts that the reviewed diff covers 'ONLY its delta' via 'robot comment/diff scope' — if the script validates this solely by inspecting the bot's own robot comment (written by the system under test), the assertion is tautological: the bot passes the test by writing any comment, regardless of whether the diff scope was actually correct. An independent check of the actual Gerrit diff range (e.g., comparing parent1..HEAD vs parent0..HEAD file sets via the REST API) is needed to avoid locking in incorrect diff-scoping behavior.
findings.location: What — Scenario 2 (Stacking); AC line 'assert via robot comment/diff scope'
findings.suggested_fix: In reviewbot-e2e.sh (or the new scenario script), after the bot votes, call `curl .../changes/{id}/revisions/current/files` and assert that no file modified only in the parent stories appears in the diff. This is independent of the bot's comment and directly verifies the boundary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Scenario 2: add an independent diff-scope assertion — after the bot votes, query `GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/files` and assert the returned file set matches only the stacking agent's own commit files (not the parent stories' files) rather than relying solely on the bot's robot comment.
findings.finding: Scenario 2 (Stacking) asserts 'reviewed diff covers ONLY its delta (assert via robot comment/diff scope)' but names no independent verification protocol for the diff boundary. The `reviewbot-e2e.sh` helper `bot_max_vote()` reads only the integer vote value; the script has no helper for verifying WHICH files were in scope for the review. The only observable named is the bot's own robot comment — written by the system under test — making the assertion potentially tautological. No decision rule for independently confirming diff scope (e.g., query `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/files` and assert the file set excludes parent-story paths) is co-located with the robot-comment verification schema.
findings.location: What — Scenario 2 (Stacking); AC line 'Scenario script committed alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `assert_diff_scope` helper to the new scenario script: call `api_get /changes/${CHANGE_NUM}/revisions/current/files`, parse the JSON file map keys (excluding /COMMIT_MSG), and assert none of the parent-story changed paths appear in the list. This is a pure REST read requiring no extra permissions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a per-scenario failure-isolation policy for the new compound script: specify whether each scenario runs independently (with its own harness/pass/blocked exit recording) or the script aborts on first failure, and define the exit-code vocabulary (0=all pass, 2=harness failure, 3=at least one scenario blocked) so the epic close gate has an unambiguous pass criterion.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no per-scenario failure-isolation policy for the new 7-scenario compound script. `reviewbot-e2e.sh` is a single-scenario script using `set -euo pipefail` with `exit 2` (HARNESS-FAILURE) / `exit 3` (GATE-BLOCKED) — any failure aborts the whole run. The new script covers 7 dependent scenarios (scenarios 1–3 are explicitly a RED/BLUE/GREEN replay sequence). Whether a failure in scenario 3 aborts scenarios 4–7, or the script continues and reports a partial pass, is unspecified — as is the exit-code vocabulary for a multi-scenario run.
findings.location: AC line 'Scenario script committed alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh (reusing its helpers where sensible)'; What — all 7 scenarios

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concurrency model for Scenarios 1 and 7 in the script: how agents are launched (background subshells, separate processes), how the script waits for all parallel operations to complete (e.g., `wait $PID1 $PID2`), and what constitutes a valid synchronized outcome check.
findings.finding: The orchestration of parallel agents in Scenario 1 (and concurrent submits in Scenario 7) has no specified synchronization mechanism in the script: there is no description of how the script launches agents, waits for both to complete their push, and then verifies outcomes atomically, leaving a potential TOCTOU where outcome checks run before the second agent has finished.
findings.location: Scenario 1 (Parallel siblings) / Scenario 7 (Races + negatives)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Scenario 7 (reconciler backfill): specify the concrete mechanism for simulating a dropped webhook — e.g., push the change while the receiver's webhook endpoint is temporarily blocked (iptables rule on the Gerrit outbound webhook call, or a temporary webhooks.config disable), then re-enable and wait for the reconciler's next poll interval to backfill and cast the vote, asserting the `reconcile_done` log event shows `reviewed >= 1`.
findings.finding: Scenario 7's reconciler-backfill sub-scenario ('reconciler backfills a dropped webhook for a feature-branch change INCLUDING a merge change') has no specified mechanism for creating and verifying the 'dropped webhook' precondition while the review-bot is running continuously and processing all events within its reconcile interval.
findings.location: What section — Scenario 7 (Races + negatives); last bullet of the scenario description

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether the S5 scenario script can `source` `reviewbot-e2e.sh` helpers or confirm the plan means the helpers will be duplicated/inlined; if sourcing is intended, refactor `reviewbot-e2e.sh` to add a sourcing guard (`[[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "$0" ]] || return 0`) and document that in the AC.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the new S5 scenario script will be 'scripted alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh, reusing its helpers where sensible', but reading the implementation file shows reviewbot-e2e.sh defines only internal-only functions (`log`, `pass`, `harness`, `blocked`, `bot_max_vote`, `api_get`) with no `export`, no sourcing guard, and no lib/include structure — there is no mechanism to source these helpers from a sibling script without modifying reviewbot-e2e.sh first.
findings.location: What section: 'Scripted alongside `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`'; AC line: 'Scenario script committed alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh (reusing its helpers where sensible)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `[[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "$0" ]] || return 0` near the top of `reviewbot-e2e.sh` (after env-var declarations, before the `set -euo pipefail` body), allowing a sibling script to `source infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh` and inherit `log`, `pass`, `harness`, `blocked`, `api_get` as sourced functions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC3's narrative claims with concrete proving commands: (a) define 'attested stories' (e.g., change IDs that received LLM-Review +1 before the parallel-submit sequence); (b) add a script step that queries Gerrit REST (`/changes/<id>/reviewers` or `/changes/<id>/votes`) to assert no vote wipe occurred; (c) assert no rebase via `git log --oneline` on the submitted branch; (d) specify what 'single atomic landing' means (one Gerrit submit event? one merge commit? one GitHub push?)
findings.finding: AC3 states 'RED/BLUE/GREEN replay (1–3) shows zero vote wipes, zero manual rebases, zero re-reviews of attested stories, single atomic landing' but provides no proving command or script reference that would produce evidence for any of these four sub-claims. The term 'attested stories' is also undefined — it is unclear whether it means changes that already received LLM-Review +1, changes listed in a specific manifest, or something else.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 / 'RED/BLUE/GREEN replay (1–3)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Scenario 4: add a carry-provenance assertion — after the re-merge push, query `GET /a/changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_LABELS` and assert the LLM-Review vote entry carries `copied: true` (Gerrit's per-vote carry flag) to prove the vote was copied rather than re-cast, distinguishing CARRIES from RE-RUNS.
findings.finding: Scenario 4 names three distinct vote-state outcomes — LLM-Review +1 CARRIES, Verified RE-RUNS, BOTH wiped (REWORK) — but the plan specifies no verification protocol (decision rule + REST endpoint/field) for asserting each state. The `bot_max_vote()` helper in `reviewbot-e2e.sh` returns the current integer maximum vote; it cannot distinguish a *copied* (carried) vote from a freshly re-cast one. Gerrit exposes the copy-kind in the patchset detail (`change_kind` field on the revision or the `DETAILED_LABELS` response), but no assertion against this field is described.
findings.location: What — Scenario 4 (Vote-carry on re-merge)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to Scenario 7 a specified webhook-drop simulation method (e.g., temporarily disable the Gerrit stream-events endpoint or filter, inject a synthetic gap via the reconciler's polling interval, or replay a missed event via script) AND a concrete proving command (e.g., query the reconciler log or Gerrit REST API) that confirms the backfill event was processed.
findings.finding: Scenario 7 requires testing that 'the reconciler backfills a dropped webhook for a feature-branch change INCLUDING a merge change,' but the plan specifies no mechanism for simulating a dropped webhook — no teardown command, no event-injection method, no log assertion is described. An executing agent cannot determine whether to stop the webhook listener, delete the hook registration, or inject a synthetic gap, and cannot verify the backfill completed without a specified check.
findings.location: What — Scenario 7 / 'reconciler backfills a dropped webhook'
findings.suggested_fix: In the scenario script, define a `drop_webhook` helper that (1) pauses the webhook consumer (e.g., `systemctl stop gerrit-stream-events-relay`), (2) pushes the target change, (3) restarts the consumer, then (4) polls `GET /changes/?q=change:<id>&o=DETAILED_LABELS` until both LLM-Review and Verified votes appear or a timeout fires; assert the votes are present within the SLO.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit failure-cleanup path (e.g., a shell `trap` or teardown function) to the scenario script that abandons all e2e-created changes and deletes the feature branch regardless of whether scenarios pass or fail.
findings.finding: No abort/cleanup path is specified for mid-run script failure: the retire path (branch deletion + change abandonment) only appears as a success-path AC, so a crash between scenarios leaves the shared Gerrit branch and open changes in an inconsistent state that could corrupt subsequent scenario runs or pollute the shared Gerrit instance.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Cleanup: e2e feature branch deleted; throwaway changes abandoned'
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the script body in a trap: `trap 'gerrit_abandon_all_e2e_changes; gerrit_delete_branch feature/e2e-$DATE' EXIT`; make abandon and delete idempotent (tolerate 'already abandoned' / 'branch not found' responses).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the helper-reuse mechanism: either (a) explicitly declare that reviewbot-e2e.sh will be refactored to expose a sourceable common lib before S5 (and name the lib file), or (b) explicitly accept copy-paste for the 2-script case and note the maintenance trade-off — do not leave 'reusing helpers where sensible' ambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan says the new scenario script should reuse reviewbot-e2e.sh helpers 'where sensible', but the existing script has no sourceable library form — helpers (api_get, log, pass, harness, blocked, bot_max_vote) are inline functions with exactly 1 call-site. Reuse either means copy-pasting them (cargo-cult duplication) or extracting them into a shared lib with only 2 call-sites total (below the Rule-of-Three threshold for a justified abstraction). The plan doesn't specify which approach is intended, leaving a real implementation ambiguity that risks both unnecessary duplication and premature abstraction.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Scenario script committed alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh (reusing its helpers where sensible)'
findings.suggested_fix: The simplest path: extract the shared helpers (api_get, log, pass, harness, blocked, plus a new poll_label helper for two-vote polling) into infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e-lib.sh and source it from both scripts. This gives a justified 2-consumer lib for the shared infra and keeps each script focused on its own scenario logic. If a third E2E script is anticipated (S6 cost/latency measurements), the Rule-of-Three will be met.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Scenario 4: specify the assertion mechanism that proves the vote CARRIED (not re-cast) — e.g., check the patchset's `change_kind` == `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` via `GET /changes/{id}?o=ALL_REVISIONS` and/or assert the Gerrit 'Copied Votes' message is present before the bot has had time to re-vote (or assert the bot did NOT emit a `voter_voted` log event for the new patchset).
findings.finding: Scenario 4's 'LLM-Review +1 CARRIES' assertion is mechanistically unspecified: the REST labels endpoint returns the label value whether it was carried (copyCondition satisfied, no bot re-review) or freshly cast (bot ran a new review on the same diff). The plan gives no mechanism for distinguishing them in the script, making the scenario unable to actually prove the carry behavior as opposed to a coincidentally identical re-review.
findings.location: What section — Scenario 4 (Vote-carry on re-merge); AC: 'All 7 scenarios pass live; change URLs + votes recorded on the epic ticket'
findings.suggested_fix: After pushing the re-merge patchset, before polling for the label value: (1) assert `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{ps}/related` or `GET /changes/{id}?o=ALL_REVISIONS` shows `kind == MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` for the new patchset, and (2) check the change messages list for the 'Copied Votes' substring Gerrit appends when a vote is carried — this substring is absent when the bot casts a fresh vote.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the Scope section whether the target Gerrit is a dedicated test/staging instance or a shared environment, and if shared, identify the project-level isolation boundary (e.g., isolated Gerrit project, ACL-restricted namespace) that prevents test operations from affecting other users or repositories.
findings.finding: The plan targets 'the running Gerrit' without specifying whether this is a dedicated, isolated test instance or a shared (staging/production) environment. Scenarios 7 (concurrent submit races, refused pushes, webhook drops) and 3 (atomic landing + branch deletion) involve disruptive operations that could interfere with real users or live data if executed against a shared Gerrit.
findings.location: What section ('Seven scenarios on the running Gerrit') / AC line 5 ('Cleanup: e2e feature branch deleted')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence environment declaration, e.g.: 'All scenarios run against the dedicated `gerrit-test.internal` instance (or the isolated `e2e-sandbox` project on the shared Gerrit) — not against any production project.' Reference the S1 config that provisions this boundary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update AC2 to explicitly name `docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md` as the committed evidence target alongside the epic ticket, matching the verifier requirement called out in the HANDOFF comment.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 says change URLs + votes are 'recorded on the epic ticket', but the established committed practice (HANDOFF comment, S3 evidence) uses `docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md` as the durable in-repo artifact. The AC does not name this file, so the verifier may look only at ticket comments and miss or skip the committed evidence requirement.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'All 7 scenarios pass live; change URLs + votes recorded on the epic ticket'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC2: 'All 7 scenarios pass live; change URLs + votes recorded on the epic ticket AND committed to `docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md` (S5 section) as the durable in-repo run-log artifact.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `merge_change_409_guard` log-level discrepancy: either update voter.py to emit at `logging.INFO` as shipped (and correct the epic S2 spec + runbook to say INFO, not ERROR) or change the emit level to ERROR — and ensure the S5 scenario 7 assertion targets whichever level is authoritative.
findings.finding: The plan references `merge_change_409_guard` as a named log event that should fire at ERROR level (epic S2 description: 'merge_change_409_guard (ERROR)'), and S5 scenario 7 depends on this event as a 'transparent merge detection' observable. However, voter.py line 399 emits this event at `logging.INFO`, not ERROR. The log-event level vocabulary is inconsistently specified between the plan and the shipped implementation — an executor scripting scenario 7's merge-detection assertion against the ERROR-level journald stream will never see this event.
findings.location: What — Scenario 7 ('transparent merge detection'); src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py line 399
findings.suggested_fix: In voter.py change `logging.INFO` to `logging.WARNING` or `logging.ERROR` on the `merge_change_409_guard` emit (line 399) to match the plan's stated level, since this event signals that the bare /patch path was bypassed — an operationally significant routing decision. Update the runbook and S2 spec to match. The S5 scenario script should then grep for the WARNING/ERROR-level marker to prove transparent merge detection.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State in the new scenario script's header (or in the plan's Scope/Auth section) whether parallel-agent pushes use a single admin SSH key with distinct git user.email/name configs (the minimal viable model) or distinct Gerrit service accounts — and note any provisioning step if the latter.
findings.finding: The new multi-agent scenario script's push-authentication model for parallel-worktree agents (scenarios 1 and 2) is unspecified. The existing reviewbot-e2e.sh uses a single GERRIT_ADMIN_SSH_KEY identity; the plan says 'two agents in separate worktrees' but does not state whether those agents push under the same key (differentiated only by git config user.email/name) or distinct service accounts. If distinct SSH identities are assumed but not provisioned, the script cannot run scenario 1.
findings.location: What / Scenario 1 ('two agents in separate worktrees') + Acceptance Criteria / AC2
findings.suggested_fix: The single-key model suffices for proving MERGE_IF_NECESSARY: each worktree uses the same GERRIT_ADMIN_SSH_KEY but configures distinct `git config user.email` values (e.g. agent-red@e2e and agent-blue@e2e). Add a one-liner to the script header documenting this, matching the pattern in reviewbot-e2e.sh lines 152–153.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the new scenario script's committed file path in AC1 (e.g. `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e-88ab-scenarios.sh` or similar) so the verifier has a deterministic artifact to check.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 says 'Scenario script committed alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh' but never names the new script's file path. The AC verifier cannot confirm the deliverable is present without a deterministic path to check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'Scenario script committed alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh (reusing its helpers where sensible)'
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC1 to read: 'Scenario script `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e-88ab-scenarios.sh` committed alongside `reviewbot-e2e.sh`, reusing its helpers (source or copy `api_get`, `harness`, `blocked`, `pass` functions + exit-code conventions).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to Scenario 3 a concrete replication-verification step, for example: after Gerrit submit, poll `git ls-remote <github-mirror-url> refs/heads/main` and assert the returned SHA matches the Gerrit-submitted merge commit SHA within a specified timeout.
findings.finding: Scenario 3 claims 'replication advances GitHub main with all commits' as a required behavior to verify, but no proving command or assertion is specified — the plan does not describe how to check the GitHub mirror's state (e.g., `git ls-remote`, a GitHub API call, or a comparison of SHAs).
findings.location: What — Scenario 3 (Merge-back) / 'replication advances GitHub main with all commits'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a helper `assert_github_replicated <sha> <timeout_seconds>` in `reviewbot-e2e.sh` that polls `git ls-remote` and fails with a timeout error if the SHA does not appear, then call it from Scenario 3 after submit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add an AC that specifies what the R5 ADR deliverable must contain and where it is committed, or clarify that 'ADR' here refers to an existing document (cite it) and 'apt-otter-urn' is the only live-evidence artifact needed.
findings.finding: The Scope section maps R5 to 'ADR + apt-otter-urn', implying an ADR document is a deliverable for this sprint, but no ADR is described in the What section and no AC verifies its production or content. This creates an orphan deliverable with no acceptance coverage.
findings.location: Scope section — 'Requirement mapping: … R5: ADR + apt-otter-urn'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align AC3 terminology with the What section by either (a) labeling scenarios 1–3 as RED/BLUE/GREEN in the What section, or (b) replacing 'RED/BLUE/GREEN' in AC3 with the actual scenario names ('Parallel siblings / Stacking / Merge-back').
findings.finding: AC3 introduces color labels 'RED/BLUE/GREEN replay (1–3)' that do not appear anywhere in the What section's scenario descriptions, making it unclear which scenario maps to which color and creating a verify step that references concepts not defined in the plan body.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — '- [ ] RED/BLUE/GREEN replay (1–3) shows zero vote wipes…'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC3 text with: 'Scenarios 1 (Parallel siblings) / 2 (Stacking) / 3 (Merge-back) show zero vote wipes, zero manual rebases, zero re-reviews of attested stories, single atomic landing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add per-step timeouts (and a configurable max-wait) to the scenario script so that any missing vote, CI signal, or webhook causes a loud FAIL rather than an indefinite hang.
findings.finding: The E2E script has no stated per-step timeout or failure-handling policy: if a Gerrit vote, CI signal, or webhook never arrives (e.g., bot is slow, CI queue is backed up), the script will hang indefinitely or produce a false pass, with no specified maximum wait, retry, or abort path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / Scenario script + live evidence section
findings.suggested_fix: In reviewbot-e2e.sh helpers, wrap each 'wait for vote/label' poll with a deadline (e.g., `wait_for_label LLM-Review +1 $CHANGE_ID 120` that exits non-zero after 120 s); propagate non-zero to scenario-level failure and trigger the cleanup path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify R5's observable proof: explain what 'apt-otter-urn' refers to (ticket, ADR section, or artifact), add a scenario number or AC line that produces a live, inspectable signal for R5, so every requirement has a within-plan verifiable outcome.
findings.finding: R5's end-state-observable proof is opaque: it is listed as 'ADR + apt-otter-urn' with no explanation of what 'apt-otter-urn' is, where it lives, or what observable state constitutes proof of R5 passing. If this is an external epic/ticket reference, it means R5 has no within-plan verifiable evidence and a reviewer cannot confirm R5 is satisfied from this plan alone.
findings.location: Scope section — 'Requirement mapping: … R5: ADR + apt-otter-urn'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'R5: proven by ADR §<section> (apt-otter-urn = <epic-ref>); Scenario 7 race/negative evidence serves as live corroboration; link added to epic ticket alongside other change URLs.' Then add a corresponding AC bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a section (or a note in the script header) specifying how runtime credentials (Gerrit bot token, agent SSH keys, GitHub replication token) are injected at execution time — e.g., sourced from a CI secret store, Vault, or environment variables — and confirm no credential is hardcoded or checked in.
findings.finding: The E2E script (`infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh` and the companion scenario script) must authenticate to Gerrit (bot API tokens, agent push credentials) and optionally to GitHub (replication assertions), but the plan is entirely silent on how those credentials are supplied to the script — creating a risk they are hardcoded or stored in plaintext inside the script or a checked-in vars file.
findings.location: Scope section / AC line 1 ('Scenario script committed alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh')
findings.suggested_fix: In the script preamble, assert required env vars (e.g., `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN`, `AGENT_SSH_KEY_PATH`) are set and non-empty; source them from the CI secret store / `op run` / `vault read` rather than accepting inline values. Document this contract in the plan's Scope section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a credential-handling note to the script spec: agent credentials MUST be injected via environment variables (never hardcoded), the script MUST NOT log them (set +x around auth steps or redact), and the AC should assert no secrets appear in the committed file or its CI log output.
findings.finding: The plan commits an E2E scenario script (alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh) that must authenticate to Gerrit (push changes, cast votes) and indirectly exercises GitHub replication — both boundary-crossing paths — but nowhere describes how test-agent credentials (Gerrit HTTP passwords, SSH keys, service-account tokens) are sourced, stored, or kept out of the committed script and logs.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Scenario script committed alongside reviewbot-e2e.sh'; Scope — 'Depends on S1 (config/ACLs)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC or script preamble, add: 'All agent credentials are read from environment variables (e.g. GERRIT_HTTP_PASSWORD, GITHUB_REPLICATION_TOKEN); the script enforces `set +x` around any step that expands a credential; a grep-for-secrets check (e.g. `git secrets --scan`) is run as part of the commit hook or CI gate on the script file itself.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
